source: Stage Three (v3) per-prefecture T3 covariates for WA 2014. Replaces the Stage Two country-level proxies with: (a) per-prefecture population_density from substrate pop_size (thousands) divided by hand-curated areas in km² (Guinee Statistics RGPH 2014, LISGIS NPHC 2008, Statistics Sierra Leone NPHC 2015); (b) per-prefecture road_connectivity_index hand-binned 1-5 from OSM + World Bank transport-sector inventory; (c) per-prefecture healthcare_distance_km via haversine from substrate centroid to nearest reference hospital in a hand-curated WHO HHA + national MoH facility set; (d) per-prefecture conflict_access_score hand-binned 1-3 from Faye 2015 Lancet ID (Forestiere community resistance) and WHO 2014 NEJM (Mano River cross-border response). All values cited per row in derivation_notes. Stdlib-only derivation; no external dependencies.

geographies.derivation_notes: WA 2014 prefecture in GUINEA. Area 12653 km² (Guinee Statistics RGPH 2014); population 359 K (substrate cross-checked against RGPH 2014); density 28/km². Road connectivity index 2 (interior with secondary roads only). Healthcare distance 93 km to Macenta Hospital (haversine from 8.683,-8.633). Conflict access score 3 (Forestiere community resistance per Faye 2015 Lancet ID). Per-prefecture differentiation per Stage Three methodology.
